message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  Transfer learning (TL) from pretrained deep models is a standard practice in modern medical image classification (MIC). However, what levels of features to be reused are problem-dependent, and uniformly finetuning all layers of pretrained models may be suboptimal. This insight has partly motivated the recent
                  <jats:italic>differential</jats:italic>
                  TL strategies, such as TransFusion (TF) and layer-wise finetuning (LWFT), which treat the layers in the pretrained models differentially. In this paper, we add one more strategy into this family, called
                  <jats:italic>TruncatedTL</jats:italic>
                  , which reuses and finetunes appropriate bottom layers and directly discards the remaining layers. This yields not only superior MIC performance but also compact models for efficient inference, compared to other differential TL methods. Our code is available at:
                  <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="https://github.com/sun-umn/TTL">https://github.com/sun-umn/TTL</jats:ext-link>
                  .
                </jats:p>
title: Rethinking Transfer Learning for Medical Image Classification
